problem_restatement: Ship a pumped-fn-native session frame whose complete declared tree activates at entry, whose controller branches activate after per-exec tags exist, and whose realistic issue-triage proof holds every anti-goal.
inbound_signal: The human confirmed eager complete-tree activation is a model strength and accepted runtime missing-tag failures until later static analysis.
decision_delta: Kept eager activation, recorded controller preparation as the deferred boundary, migrated provider cancellation to ExecutionContext.signal, and added scope-level plus execution-level duplicate tag collection regressions.
evidence_delta: Objective is 16 of 16. Lite is 216 of 216, SDK core is 52 of 52 excluding the managed child-process packaging harness, SDK test is 15 of 15, providers are 49 passed with 4 explicit integration skips, framework is 4 of 4, touched lint is zero, and alignment is zero-gap.

anti_goal_violation_review.claim: All fourteen anti-goal metrics read zero.
A missing required tag remains a runtime error in this frame.
anti_goal_violation_review.verdict: accepted trade-off; static analysis remains forbidden in this frame

unexpected_results.claim: The realistic two-tool test initially failed after source tests passed.
unexpected_results.verdict: stale built Lite artifact; rebuilt packages and added an execution-level regression

hallucination_sweep.claim: Claude independently approved the final branch.
hallucination_sweep.verdict: rejected; authentication and network sandbox prevented a result

dkr_decision: Complete the implementation frame and update the existing PR without release or merge.
expected_effect: One public session and agent model, shared execution cancellation, controlled tool composition, bounded queue composition, and a replayable database-backed triage example.
